name: htmlmin
documentation:
A configurable HTML Minifier with safety features.

```{image} https://travis-ci.org/mankyd/htmlmin.png?branch=master
:target: http://travis-ci.org/mankyd/htmlmin
```

Documentation: <https://htmlmin.readthedocs.io/en/latest/>

functions.name: htmlmin.decorator.htmlmin
documentation:
Minifies HTML that is returned by a function.

A simple decorator that minifies the HTML output of any function that it
decorates. It supports all the same options that {class}`htmlmin.minify` has.
With no options, it uses `minify`'s default settings:

```
@htmlmin
def foobar():
   return '   minify me!   '
```

or:

```
@htmlmin(remove_comments=True)
def foobar():
   return '   minify me!  <!-- and remove me! -->'
```

modules.name: htmlmin.python3html
documentation: General functions for HTML manipulation.

functions.name: htmlmin.python3html.escape
documentation:
Replace special characters "&", "<" and ">" to HTML-safe sequences.
If the optional flag quote is true (the default), the quotation mark
characters, both double quote (") and single quote (') characters are also
translated.

functions.name: htmlmin.python3html.unescape
documentation:
Convert all named and numeric character references (e.g. >, >,
&x3e;) in the string s to the corresponding unicode characters.
This function uses the rules defined by the HTML 5 standard
for both valid and invalid character references, and the list of
HTML 5 named character references defined in html.entities.html5.

functions.name: htmlmin.main.minify
documentation:
Minifies HTML in one shot.

```
param input

A string containing the HTML to be minified.

param remove_comments

Remove comments found in HTML. Individual comments can
be maintained by putting a ! as the first character inside the comment.
Thus:

<!-- FOO --> <!--! BAR -->

Will become simply:

<!-- BAR -->

The added exclamation is removed.

param remove_empty_space

Remove empty space found in HTML between an opening
and a closing tag and when it contains a newline or carriage return. If
whitespace is found that is only spaces and/or tabs, it will be turned into
a single space. Be careful, this can have unintended consequences.

param remove_all_empty_space

A more extreme version of
remove_empty_space, this removes all empty whitespace found between
tags. This is almost guaranteed to break your HTML unless you are very
careful.

param reduce_boolean_attributes

Where allowed by the HTML5 specification,
attributes such as 'disabled' and 'readonly' will have their value removed,
so 'disabled="true"' will simply become 'disabled'. This is generally a
good option to turn on except when JavaScript relies on the values.

param remove_optional_attribute_quotes

When True, optional quotes around
attributes are removed. When False, all attribute quotes are left intact.
Defaults to True.

param conver_charrefs

Decode character references such as & and .
to their single charater values where safe. This currently only applies to
attributes. Data content between tags will be left encoded.

param keep_pre

By default, htmlmin uses the special attribute pre to
allow you to demarcate areas of HTML that should not be minified. It removes
this attribute as it finds it. Setting this value to True tells htmlmin
to leave the attribute in the output.

param pre_tags

A list of tag names that should never be minified. You are
free to change this list as you see fit, but you will probably want to
include pre and textarea if you make any changes to the list. Note
that <script> and <style> tags are never minimized.

param pre_attr

Specifies the attribute that, when found in an HTML tag,
indicates that the content of the tag should not be minified. Defaults to
pre. You can also prefix individual tag attributes with
{pre_attr}- to prevent the contents of the individual attribute from
being changed.

return

A string containing the minified HTML.
```

If you are going to be minifying multiple HTML documents, each with the same
settings, consider using {class}`.Minifier`.

classes.name: htmlmin.main.Minifier

methods.name: htmlmin.main.Minifier.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Minifier.

See {class}`htmlmin.minify` for an explanation of options.

methods.name: htmlmin.main.Minifier.minify
documentation:
Runs HTML through the minifier in one pass.

```
param input

HTML to be fed into the minimizer. Multiple chunks of HTML
can be provided, and they are fed in sequentially as if they were
concatenated.

returns

A string containing the minified HTML.
```

This is the simplest way to use an existing `Minifier` instance. This
method takes in HTML and minfies it, returning the result. Note that this
method resets the internal state of the parser before it does any work. If
there is pending HTML in the buffers, it will be lost.

methods.name: htmlmin.main.Minifier.input
documentation:
Feed more HTML into the input stream

```
param input

HTML to be fed into the minimizer. Multiple chunks of HTML
can be provided, and they are fed in sequentially as if they were
concatenated. You can also call this method multiple times to achieve
the same effect.
```

methods.name: htmlmin.main.Minifier.output
documentation: Retrieve the minified output generated thus far.

methods.name: htmlmin.main.Minifier.finalize
documentation:
Finishes current input HTML and returns mininified result.

This method flushes any remaining input HTML and returns the minified
result. It resets the state of the internal parser in the process so that
new HTML can be minified. Be sure to call this method before you reuse
the `Minifier` instance on a new HTML document.

An object that supports HTML Minification.

Options are passed into this class at initialization time and are then
persisted across each use of the instance. If you are going to be minifying
multiple peices of HTML, this will be more efficient than using
{class}`htmlmin.minify`.

See {class}`htmlmin.minify` for an explanation of options.

modules.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser
documentation: A parser for HTML and XHTML.

classes.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.__init__
documentation:
Initialize and reset this instance.

If convert_charrefs is True (the default), all character references
are automatically converted to the corresponding Unicode characters.

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.reset
documentation: Reset this instance.  Loses all unprocessed data.

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.feed
documentation:
Feed data to the parser.

Call this as often as you want, with as little or as much text
as you want (may include '\n').

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.close
documentation: Handle any buffered data.

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.get_starttag_text
documentation: Return full source of start tag: '<...>'.

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.goahead
documentation: Internal -- handle data as far as reasonable.  May leave state
and data to be processed by a subsequent call.  If 'end' is
true, force handling all data as if followed by EOF marker.

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.parse_html_declaration
documentation: Internal -- parse html declarations, return length or -1 if not terminated
See w3.org/TR/html5/tokenization.html#markup-declaration-open-state
See also parse_declaration in _markupbase

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.parse_bogus_comment
documentation: Internal -- parse bogus comment, return length or -1 if not terminated
see http://www.w3.org/TR/html5/tokenization.html#bogus-comment-state

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.parse_pi
documentation: Internal -- parse processing instr, return end or -1 if not terminated

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.parse_starttag
documentation: Internal -- handle starttag, return end or -1 if not terminated

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.check_for_whole_start_tag
documentation: Internal -- check to see if we have a complete starttag; return end
or -1 if incomplete.

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.parse_endtag
documentation: Internal -- parse endtag, return end or -1 if incomplete

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.handle_startendtag
documentation: Overridable -- finish processing of start+end tag: <tag.../>

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.handle_starttag
documentation: Overridable -- handle start tag

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.handle_endtag
documentation: Overridable -- handle end tag

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.handle_charref
documentation: Overridable -- handle character reference

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.handle_entityref
documentation: Overridable -- handle entity reference

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.handle_data
documentation: Overridable -- handle data

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.handle_comment
documentation: Overridable -- handle comment

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.handle_decl
documentation: Overridable -- handle declaration

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.handle_pi
documentation: Overridable -- handle processing instruction

methods.name: htmlmin.python3html.parser.HTMLParser.unescape
documentation: Internal -- helper to remove special character quoting

Find tags and other markup and call handler functions.

Usage:
    p = HTMLParser()
    p.feed(data)
    ...
    p.close()

Start tags are handled by calling self.handle_starttag() or
self.handle_startendtag(); end tags by self.handle_endtag().  The
data between tags is passed from the parser to the derived class
by calling self.handle_data() with the data as argument (the data
may be split up in arbitrary chunks).  If convert_charrefs is
True the character references are converted automatically to the
corresponding Unicode character (and self.handle_data() is no
longer split in chunks), otherwise they are passed by calling
self.handle_entityref() or self.handle_charref() with the string
containing respectively the named or numeric reference as the
argument.

classes.name: htmlmin.middleware.HTMLMinMiddleware
documentation:
WSGI Middleware that minifies html on the way out.

```
param by_default

Specifies if minification should be turned on or off by
default. Defaults to True.

param keep_header

The middleware recognizes one custom HTTP header that
can be used to turn minification on or off on a per-request basis:
X-HTML-Min-Enable. Setting the header to true will turn minfication
on; anything else will turn minification off. If by_default is set to
False, this header is how you would turn minification back on. The
middleware, by default, removes the header from the output. Setting this
to True leaves the header in tact.

param debug

A quick setting to turn all minification off. The middleware
is effectively bypassed.
```

This simple middleware minifies any HTML content that passes through it. Any
additional keyword arguments beyond the three settings the middleware has are
passed on to the internal minifier. The documentation for the options can
be found under {class}`htmlmin.minify`.
